SECTION 1  
A squirrel is sitting in front of the second hollow. It says: "There are no nuts in the other hollow." This statement implies that the other hollow (the first one) has no nuts. If the squirrel is a red squirrel, it tells the truth, so the first hollow has no nuts. If it's a gray squirrel, it lies, so the first hollow does have nuts.

SECTION 2  
The squirrel also says: "There is at least one nut in one of the hollows." This is a true statement, as it's implied that the first hollow has nuts (if the squirrel is gray) or has no nuts (if the squirrel is red). Since the squirrel is making a statement that is true in either case, it must be a red squirrel.

SECTION 3  
The squirrel is a red squirrel because it makes a true statement. If it were a gray squirrel, it would be lying, and the statement "There is at least one nut in one of the hollows" would be false, which contradicts the actual situation.

SECTION 4  
The squirrel is red. It is telling the truth about the first hollow having no nuts, and it is also telling the truth about the presence of at least one nut in one of the hollows. This confirms that the squirrel is a red squirrel.

SECTION 5  
The squirrel is red. It is not a gray squirrel because its statements are true. A gray squirrel would have made a false statement, which would contradict the actual situation.

SECTION 6  
The squirrel is red. It is not a gray squirrel because it tells the truth about the nuts in the hollows. This confirms that the squirrel is a red squirrel.
